MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nils Rosenquest, Esq., ROSENQUEST & ASSOCIATES, 415-292-0980, rosenquest@earthlink.net, for Plaintiff Juana Lorena Hernandez,
Warren Klein, Esq, Law Office of Santana, Vierra, Stevenson, Harris & Hermanson, (415) 777-1308, Warren.Klein@LibertyMutual.com, for Defendants CITY OF SAN FRANSCISCO and
COUNTY OF SAN FRANSCISCO, BAYVIEW PLAZA, LLP.,

The court received Defendants notice of constest to the Court's tentative ruling is untimly. Defendants contest email was received at 5:30 p.m. Court allows argument to move forward.

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JUANA HERNANDEZ'S MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION OF CASE.

Plaintiff's "motion to reclassify action from limited to unlimited jurisdiction" is granted. Defendant's late-filed opposition was not considered. (See CRC 3.1300(d); Bozzi v. Nordstrom, Inc. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 755, 765 ["A trial court has broad discretion under rule 3.1300(d) of the Rules of Court to refuse to consider papers served and filed beyond the deadline without a prior court order finding good cause for late submission."].)

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)
